ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the device is installed */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., Sensor, Actuator) */,
  "install_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was installed */,
  "maintenance_schedule" TEXT /* Scheduled maintenance date for the device */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was last calibrated */,
  "device_status" TEXT /* Current status of the device (e.g., Active, Inactive, Maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_series_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device that collected the data */,
  "timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the data measurement */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the measurement */,
  "anomaly" INTEGER /* Indicates if there is an anomaly (0 = no, 1 = yes) */,
  "changepoint" INTEGER /* Indicates if there is a changepoint (0 = no, 1 = yes) */,
  "trend" REAL /* Calculated trend value */,
  "noise" REAL /* Calculated noise level */,
  "seasonality1" REAL /* First seasonal component */,
  "seasonality2" REAL /* Second seasonal component */,
  "seasonality3" REAL /* Third seasonal component */,
  "data_quality_score" REAL /* Data quality score for the measurement */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_time_series_data_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly record */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated time series data */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of anomaly detected */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the anomaly */,
  "detected_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the anomaly was detected */,
  "resolved_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the anomaly was resolved */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Notes on how the anomaly was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "changepoints" (
  "changepoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each changepoint record */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated time series data */,
  "changepoint_type" TEXT /* Type of changepoint detected */,
  "timestamp" INTEGER /* Timestamp of the changepoint */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changepoint */,
  "impact_assessment" TEXT /* Assessment of the impact of the changepoint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("changepoint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_changepoints_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trends" (
  "trend_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each trend record */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated time series data */,
  "trend_value" REAL /* Calculated trend value */,
  "calculation_date" TEXT /* Date when the trend was calculated */,
  "trend_type" TEXT /* Type of trend detected (e.g., Upward, Downward, Stable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("trend_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_trends_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "seasonal_patterns" (
  "pattern_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each seasonal pattern record */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated time series data */,
  "seasonality1" REAL /* First seasonal component */,
  "seasonality2" REAL /* Second seasonal component */,
  "seasonality3" REAL /* Third seasonal component */,
  "calculation_date" TEXT /* Date when the seasonal patterns were calculated */,
  "pattern_type" TEXT /* Type of seasonal pattern detected (e.g., Daily, Weekly, Monthly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pattern_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_seasonal_patterns_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Analyst, Engineer, Admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, download) */,
  "resource_accessed" TEXT /* Resource accessed by the user (e.g., report, dashboard) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated anomaly, if any */,
  "changepoint_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated changepoint, if any */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message detailing the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_status" TEXT /* Status of the alert (e.g., Open, Closed, In Progress) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_anomaly_id FOREIGN KEY ("anomaly_id") REFERENCES anomalies ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_changepoint_id FOREIGN KEY ("changepoint_id") REFERENCES changepoints ("changepoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the configuration was last updated */,
  "config_type" TEXT /* Type of configuration (e.g., System, User, Device) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data quality record */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated time series data */,
  "missing_values" REAL /* Percentage of missing values in the dataset */,
  "data_consistency" REAL /* Data consistency score */,
  "data_accuracy" REAL /* Data accuracy score */,
  "data_reliability" REAL /* Data reliability score */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id")
)
